I hope I followed all guidelines for contributing, this is my first contribution. 

There is a bug in `Base.GMP.MPZ.invert` it returns GMP return code, instead of the actual value. This PR fixes it. I am not sure if it could be implemented better, but from my testing you cannot get away without a deepcopy.

Before:
```
julia> Base.GMP.MPZ.invert(big"3", big"7")
1
```

After:
```
julia> Base.GMP.MPZ.invert(big"3", big"7")
5

I hope I followed all guidelines for contributing, this is my first contribution. 

There is a bug in `Base.GMP.MPZ.invert` it returns GMP return code, instead of the actual value. This PR fixes it. I am not sure if it could be implemented better, but from my testing you cannot get away without a deepcopy.

Before:
```
julia> Base.GMP.MPZ.invert(big"3", big"7")
1
```

After:
```
julia> Base.GMP.MPZ.invert(big"3", big"7")
5

However, the results may be wrong if methods with type parameters are involved. Here is an example:
```
module M

export f

"doc for f"
function f end

"doc for f(x, y)"
f(x, y) = 1

"doc for f(x::T, y)"
f(x::T, y) where T = one(T)

end

using .M
```
Then
```
help?> f(1, 1)
  doc for f(x, y)
----------------------
  doc for f(x::T, y)
```
as expected (the docstrings for all matching signatures are displayed), but also
```
help?> f(1)
  doc for f(x::T, y)
```
which appears wrong.

A look at the entry for `f` in the `META` dictionary
```
julia> first(getfield(M, Docs.META))
Pair{Any, Any}(Main.M.f, Base.Docs.MultiDoc(Type[Union{}, Tuple{Any, Any}, Union{Tuple{T}, Tuple{T, Any}} where T], IdDict{Any, Any}(Union{Tuple{T}, Tuple{T, Any}} where T => Base.Docs.DocStr(svec("doc for f(x::T, y)"), doc for f(x::T, y)
, Dict{Symbol, Any}(:typesig => Union{Tuple{T}, Tuple{T, Any}} where T, :module => Main.M, :linenumber => 11, :binding => Main.M.f, :path => "REPL[1]")), Tuple{Any, Any} => Base.Docs.DocStr(svec("doc for f(x, y)"), doc for f(x, y)
, Dict{Symbol, Any}(:typesig => Tuple{Any, Any}, :module => Main.M, :linenumber => 8, :binding => Main.M.f, :path => "REPL[1]")), Union{} => Base.Docs.DocStr(svec("doc for f"), nothing, Dict{Symbol, Any}(:typesig => Union{}, :module => Main.M, :linenumber => 5, :binding => Main.M.f, :path => "REPL[1]")))))
```
shows that the call signatures `Union{}`, `Tuple{Any, Any}` and `Union{Tuple{T}, Tuple{T, Any}} where T` are registered. The third one probably causes the strange result above.

I have found the following possibly related docstring in the source code:

https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2b2f5344138f2b50ee91132592733743f33c5358/base/docs/Docs.jl#L191-L209

As far as I can tell, the example is correct. However, in the light of my example above, the reason that `Tuple{T}` appears in `Union{Tuple{T, Any}, Tuple{T}} where T` seems to be that `T` is a type parameter and not that `f(x::T)` is a valid signature. I doubt that this was not intended.

Tested on Julia 1.10.0.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52669/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52669/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2104979654","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52669#issuecomment-2104979654","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52669","id":2104979654,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM59d3DG","user":{"login":"thofma","id":11231648,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExMjMxNjQ4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11231648?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma","html_url":"https://github.com/thofma","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-10T17:13:32Z","updated_at":"2024-05-10T17:13:32Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Is this a duplicate of https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/29437?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2104979654/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

1.9.4.

```
*(A, B::AbstractMatrix, C)
  A * B * C * D

  Chained multiplication of 3 or 4 matrices is done in the most efficient sequence, based on the sizes of the
  arrays. That is, the number of scalar multiplications needed for (A * B) * C (with 3 dense matrices) is
  compared to that for A * (B * C) to choose which of these to execute.

  If the last factor is a vector, or the first a transposed vector, then it is efficient to deal with these
  first. In particular x' * B * y means (x' * B) * y for an ordinary column-major B::Matrix. Unlike dot(x, B,
  y), this allocates an intermediate array.

  If the first or last factor is a number, this will be fused with the matrix multiplication, using 5-arg mul!.

  See also muladd, dot.

  │ Julia 1.7
  │
  │  These optimisations require at least Julia 1.7.
```

Obviously, this transformation slightly changes results for floating point numbers due to their mild non-associativity. However, it is also applied in contexts with mathematics that are decisively non-associative. For example:
```julia-repl
julia> using Octonions

julia> x = Octonion(1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8); y = Octonion(1,-2,3,4,5,6,7,8); z = Octonion(1,2,-3,4,5,6,7,8);

julia> [x;;] * [y;;] * [z;;] # left-associative evaluation
1×1 Matrix{Octonion{Int64}}:
 Octonion{Int64}(-652, -1064, 612, -736, -860, -1128, -1036, -1712)

julia> [x;;] * [y;;] * [z;;] * [1;] # trailing vector causes right-associativity
1-element Vector{Octonion{Int64}}:
 Octonion{Int64}(-652, -1064, 612, -736, -1148, -888, -1420, -1376)
```

We like to compose packages in Julia. Arbitrary re-assocation is incompatible with this. Since "standard" associativity is undefined, it becomes necessary for a "correct" package to explicitly force every association with parentheses. N-ary evaluation cannot be correctly applied without tight control of the context that ensures re-association is mathematically valid. The presence of re-association in any insufficiently-controlled context (like the above matrix-chain specialization) will lead to correctness issues across the ecosystem.

Spiritually related to #52333.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52392/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52392/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1841223749","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52392#issuecomment-1841223749","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52392","id":1841223749,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5tvthF","user":{"login":"thofma","id":11231648,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExMjMxNjQ4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11231648?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma","html_url":"https://github.com/thofma","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-12-05T16:54:45Z","updated_at":"2023-12-05T16:54:45Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Yes, quaternions for example.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1841223749/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

dir","user":{"login":"jakobnissen","id":23193950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzMTkzOTUw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/23193950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen","html_url":"https://github.com/jakobnissen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2022-11-30T08:54:02Z","updated_at":"2023-01-13T08:59:16Z","closed_at":"2022-12-14T19:19:28Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/pulls/510","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/pull/510","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/pull/510.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/pull/510.patch","merged_at":"2022-12-14T19:19:28Z"},"body":"Old behaviour would pick the first segment of JULIA_DEPOT_PATH to find the juliaup dir. This commit finds the first segment with a juliaup/juliaup.json file. If no such are found, it picks the first segment. Empty segments are expanded to the default path (which is ~/.julia/juliaup). If JULIA_DEPOT_PATH is not set, or it is set to the empty string, it will also use the default path.

__NOTE__: Julia docs on `JULIA_DEPOT_PATH` says that if the variable is set to the empty string, it means `DEPOT_PATH` is empty. It would be nice to be consistent with Base, and have it so an empty JULIA_DEPOT_PATH would resolve to no path instead of the default. However, this won't work for Juliaup, since it needs a directory to check for new versions. Hence, an empty variable just returns the default instead.

Note also that this approach does _not_ solve #257. If I understand that issue correctly, @jlapeyre wants Juliaup to search in the default depot path, even if that is not included in `JULIA_DEPOT_PATH`, such  that Julia can be forced to install _packages_ in a custom location, without forcing Juliaup to update to the same custom location. To solve this issue, we need to come up with a principled strategy for which directories Juliaup works in.

This might not solve #276. The user can set the env variable to `my_own_dir:systemwide_juliaup_dir`, and Juliaup will correctly pick the systemwide directory. However, the package manager will also pick that, if packages are not found in the self directory.

It might be necessary to have the Juliaup directory be separate from the package directory. Though we would need to think about exactly how. As I see it, there are two use cases:

* Common use case: User just wants to use their normal depot path both to load and install packages, and to use Juliaup and install new versions. This is what Juliaup already does.
* Uncommon use case: There is a systemwide `Juliaup` install, where individual users don't have (or should have) write permissions. Users want Juliaup to find versions in that location, but does not want Juliaup to begin writing to that location, since that would result in a permission error. Packages should be installed to a user-specific directory.

It seems to me a solution could be to have a specific env variable for Juliaup, independent of JULIA_DEPOT_PATH. Juliaup would then, if this variable is set, only look at that path. If it is not set, it would do as it does now.

Closes #323","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/510/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/510/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/comments/1381501737","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/pull/510#issuecomment-1381501737","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/510","id":1381501737,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEE6KR85SWAsp","user":{"login":"thofma","id":11231648,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExMjMxNjQ4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11231648?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma","html_url":"https://github.com/thofma","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-01-13T08:59:16Z","updated_at":"2023-01-13T08:59:16Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hm, does that fix the problem? having read https://docs.julialang.org/en/latest/manual/types/#man-custom-pretty-printing-1 I was confused as to why displaying my type `p::MyType` and a vector of such object `[p, p]` both appeared to call the same three-arg method of `show`. It appears that if both three and two-arg methods 
```julia
Base.show(io::IO, p::MyType)
Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/plain", p::MyType)
```
are defined, a check is performed to see whether or not 
```
Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/plain", p::MyType)
```
actually prints on several lines or not, and the 
```julia
Base.show(io::IO, p::MyType)
```
is only called if the printed string spans multiple lines. This caused me quite a bit of confusion and I wonder if this is the intended behavior and needs better documentation, or if it's a bug.

Below is an example that demonstrates what's confusing.

```julia
julia> struct MyType
           a
       end

julia> function Base.show(io::IO, p::MyType)
           print(io, "2-arg: ", p.a)
       end

julia> function Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/plain", p::MyType) 
           print(io, "3-arg: ", p.a)
       end

julia> p = MyType(1)
3-arg: 1

julia> [p,p] # This still calles 3-arg, which confused me
2-element Array{MyType,1}:
 3-arg: 1
 3-arg: 1

julia> function Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/plain", p::MyType) 
           print(io, "3-arg with newline:\n", p.a)
       end

julia> p = MyType(1)
3-arg with newline:
1

julia> [p,p] # Now the 2-arg method is called
2-element Array{MyType,1}:
 2-arg: 1
 2-arg: 1
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36253/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36253/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1003755837","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36253#issuecomment-1003755837","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36253","id":1003755837,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM471Bk9","user":{"login":"thofma","id":11231648,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExMjMxNjQ4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11231648?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma","html_url":"https://github.com/thofma","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/thofma/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-01-02T18:24:01Z","updated_at":"2022-01-02T18:24:01Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> A dup of #36072? #36076 already clarifies this a lot in the docstrings:
